skill.name: hybrid-cloud-architect
skill.description: Expert hybrid cloud architect specializing in complex multi-cloud solutions across AWS/Azure/GCP and private clouds (OpenStack/VMware). Masters hybrid connectivity, workload placement optimization, edge computing, and cross-cloud automation. Handles compliance, cost optimization, disaster recovery, and migration strategies.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for designing and managing hybrid multi-cloud infrastructure across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private clouds
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. The skill provides expert guidance for hybrid cloud architecture across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private cloud platforms. No security patterns detected. Safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Get expert guidance for designing, implementing, and managing complex hybrid cloud environments across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private clouds. This skill provides comprehensive support for hybrid connectivity, workload placement, compliance, and cost optimization.
actual_capabilities: Design hybrid cloud architectures across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private clouds (OpenStack, VMware)
Plan hybrid connectivity strategies using Direct Connect, ExpressRoute, Cloud Interconnect, and VPN solutions
Optimize workload placement based on cost, performance, compliance, and latency requirements
Implement Infrastructure as Code using Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM templates, and Pulumi
Design multi-cloud Kubernetes environments with EKS, AKS, GKE, and OpenShift integration
Create disaster recovery and business continuity strategies across hybrid environments
Implement FinOps practices for multi-cloud cost optimization and governance
Plan migration strategies from on-premises to hybrid cloud architectures
limitations: Provides guidance and recommendations only, does not directly provision cloud resources
Cannot access real-time cloud pricing, service availability, or account-specific configurations
Requires users to have familiarity with basic cloud concepts and infrastructure terminology
Recommendations should be validated against specific organizational requirements and constraints

use_cases.title: Enterprise Hybrid Cloud Design
use_cases.description: Design comprehensive hybrid cloud architecture that integrates on-premises infrastructure with multiple public cloud providers, ensuring compliance, security, and optimal workload placement.

use_cases.title: Multi-Cloud Migration Strategy
use_cases.description: Plan and execute migration from legacy on-premises systems to hybrid multi-cloud environments with minimal downtime and risk mitigation.

use_cases.title: Hybrid Cost Optimization
use_cases.description: Implement FinOps practices to optimize costs across hybrid environments, analyze workload placement economics, and establish governance frameworks.
use_cases.target_user: FinOps practitioners and cloud cost managers

prompt_templates.title: Design Hybrid Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hybrid cloud architecture for [industry/company type] that needs [specific requirements like compliance, latency, data sovereignty]. Include connectivity, security, and workload placement recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with hybrid cloud design

prompt_templates.title: Compare Cloud Providers for Workload
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare AWS, Azure, and GCP for [specific workload type]. Consider factors like cost, performance, available services, and hybrid integration capabilities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating cloud providers for specific needs

prompt_templates.title: Plan Hybrid Connectivity
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hybrid connectivity strategy between [on-premises data center/edge locations] and [AWS/Azure/GCP]. Include redundancy, bandwidth requirements, and security considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Disaster Recovery Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-site disaster recovery strategy for [application type] across [cloud providers and on-premises]. Specify RTO/RPO requirements and failover mechanisms.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning business continuity and disaster recovery

output_examples.input: Design a hybrid cloud architecture for a financial services company that needs to keep customer data on-premises for compliance but wants to use cloud for compute scalability.
output_examples.output: For your financial services hybrid architecture, I recommend: 1) On-premises: Customer database with encrypted storage, 2) Cloud (AWS/Azure): Application compute tier with autoscaling, 3) Connectivity: AWS Direct Connect or Azure ExpressRoute with dedicated bandwidth, 4) Security: End-to-end encryption with customer-managed keys (KMS), 5) Compliance: Implement SOC2 and PCI-DSS controls across both environments, 6) Data flow: Minimal data movement to cloud, only processed results returned. This keeps regulated data on-premises while leveraging cloud elasticity for compute-intensive operations.

output_examples.input: Compare AWS, Azure, and GCP for running Kubernetes workloads in a hybrid setup.
output_examples.output: For hybrid Kubernetes: AWS EKS offers deepest integration with AWS services and strong networking via VPC CNI. Azure AKS provides excellent Azure AD integration and simple setup. GCP GKE has the best managed control plane and auto-upgrades. For hybrid with on-premises: All three work well with Anthos (GCP), Azure Arc, or AWS EKS Anywhere. OpenShift provides consistency across all three clouds plus on-premises. Recommendation: If standardizing on one cloud ecosystem, choose that provider's managed service. For maximum flexibility, consider Anthos or OpenShift for consistent operations across environments.

output_examples.input: Create a disaster recovery strategy for our e-commerce platform across AWS and on-premises.
output_examples.output: Multi-site DR strategy for e-commerce: 1) Active-active configuration: Primary in AWS us-east-1, secondary in on-premises data center, 2) Database: Multi-master replication with conflict resolution, 3) Session state: Distributed cache (Redis Cluster) spanning both sites, 4) DNS: Route53 with health-based routing for automatic failover, 5) Data replication: Continuous async replication with RPO < 5 minutes, 6) RTO target: < 15 minutes via auto-scaling group pre-warming, 7) Testing: Quarterly DR drills including customer traffic redirection, 8) Backup: Cross-cloud backup to Azure GRS for ransomware protection. This approach provides high availability while protecting against regional outages.

best_practices: Always start by clarifying your compliance requirements, data sovereignty needs, and regulatory constraints before designing hybrid architecture
Implement consistent identity and access management across all environments using federation (SAML, OAuth, Active Directory)
Use Infrastructure as Code for all deployments to ensure consistency and enable disaster recovery across hybrid environments
Design for vendor flexibility by adopting multi-cloud strategies and avoiding proprietary service lock-in where possible
Implement comprehensive monitoring and observability across all environments before migrating critical workloads
Plan disaster recovery and business continuity from the beginning, not as an afterthought
anti_patterns: Treating hybrid cloud as temporary - design for long-term operations with proper automation and governance from day one
Replicating entire data centers - instead, architect for data gravity and place workloads where data naturally resides
Ignoring network costs - hybrid connectivity and data transfer charges can exceed compute savings if not properly planned
Manual environment configuration - always use Infrastructure as Code to prevent configuration drift across environments
Assuming uniform security - implement zero-trust principles and verify security posture across all environments continuously
Migrating without modernizing - use migration as an opportunity to refactor and optimize for cloud-native patterns

faq.question: What is hybrid cloud architecture?
faq.answer: Hybrid cloud architecture combines on-premises infrastructure with one or more public cloud services, allowing applications and data to move between environments. This approach provides flexibility, compliance options, and optimization for different workloads.

faq.question: When should I use hybrid cloud vs. single cloud provider?
faq.answer: Use hybrid cloud when you have compliance requirements requiring on-premises data, need to avoid vendor lock-in, want to leverage existing infrastructure investments, or require edge computing capabilities. Single cloud is simpler when you need rapid deployment and have no compliance constraints.

faq.question: How do I connect on-premises data centers to public clouds?
faq.answer: Primary options include dedicated connections (AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, Google Cloud Interconnect) for high-bandwidth, low-latency connections, or site-to-site VPN for lower bandwidth needs. SD-WAN solutions can provide intelligent routing across multiple connection types.

faq.question: What tools work best for multi-cloud Infrastructure as Code?
faq.answer: Terraform/OpenTofu is the most popular choice for multi-cloud IaC with providers for all major platforms. Pulumi offers modern programming languages for infrastructure definition. For platform-specific needs, use CloudFormation (AWS), ARM/Bicep (Azure), or Deployment Manager (GCP).

faq.question: How do I manage costs across multiple cloud providers?
faq.answer: Implement FinOps practices with centralized cost monitoring, tagging strategies for cost allocation, and regular reviews of workload placement. Use cloud provider tools (AWS Cost Explorer, Azure Cost Management, GCP Billing) alongside third-party tools for multi-cloud visibility and optimization recommendations.

faq.question: Can I run Kubernetes across hybrid environments?
faq.answer: Yes, you can run Kubernetes across hybrid environments using managed services (EKS, AKS, GKE) with on-premises clusters, or use hybrid platforms like Red Hat OpenShift, Google Anthos, or Azure Arc for consistent operations. Service mesh (Istio, Linkerd) enables secure communication across multi-cluster environments.
